data.title: Trump Signs Executive Orders to Revamp U.S. Coal Industry
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup 




  President Trump signed executive orders Tuesday aimed at revamping the U.S. coal industry and using more of the fuel to generate electricity. 



  Speaking at an event at the White House, Trump said his administration will streamline permitting and expedite leases for coal mining on federal lands. He said he was directing Energy Secretary Chris Wright to use billions of dollars in federal funding to invest in next generation coal technology, allowing the fuel to be used in an environmentally friendly way. 



  "We're going to put the miners back to work," Trump said. 



  The executive orders include ending the Department of the Interior's moratorium on new coal leases on federal land, measures to protect coal-fired power plants from closure, and promotion of power grid reliability. 



  The use of coal to generate electricity in the U.S. has been falling for years, and has been overtaken by renewable sources such as solar and wind power. U.S. coal production fell to 511.7 million tons last year from 578 million tons in 2023, and is projected to fall to 483 million tons this year, according to the U.S. Energy Information Administration's March Short Term Energy Outlook. 



  The EIA estimated that 16% of U.S. electricity would be generated using coal this year, unchanged from 2024, and that coal use would slip to 15% in 2026. Natural gas is expected to account for 40% of generation this year, down from 42% in 2024 when gas prices were low, while renewable sources are seen rising to 25% from 23% last year. 





  Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  April 08, 2025 18:00 ET (22:00 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: 11 ways to prepare for a recession - from financial pros who've survived previous economic downturns
data.description:
By Beth Pinsker

For those worried about retirement account balances, paying for groceries and the future in general

I didn't know I was launching the personal finance site WalletPop.com into the black hole of a recession in the summer of 2008, but by the fall of that year, the country was in the thick of it and so was my staff along with it.

To find my crew of freelancers, and a few full-timers, who would write about anything and everything that pertained to money, I could pick from a huge selection of laid-off journalists and other writers - and even a poet. At one point, we had six people who had been part of Pulitzer-winning teams in previous jobs. AOL directed its home-page traffic hose to our stories about beloved brands going out of business, stacking coupons and budget travel and millions of visitors scrolled through our tips on making the best of a bad few years.

The country came out of its bad economic times, but our story didn't have a happy ending. By the start of 2011, AOL's ownership changed hands, and WalletPop.com went bust, for the most part, and the contributors scattered.

Now, as the economic skies darken and serious fears about another recession take hold, I reached out to my former colleagues for their best advice from back then, and how those lessons apply to our situation today.

Jean Chatzky, bestselling author and founder of HerMoney, which now includes InvestingFixx.com:

I've been thinking about history. Since I started reporting on finances, 1987 happened, then the dot-com bubble and burst, 9/11, the 2007-'09 recession and financial crisis, COVID, now this. Throughout all of these events and the market ups and downs that they created, the strategy of staying invested, continuing to buy at all levels, and trying to tune out the noise has served investors well.

That's what I'll be doing. And I'll also be laser focused on controlling what we actually can control: our spending, our saving, our emotions (though it's hard), keeping our LinkedIn profiles and résumés fresh and continuing to network. This is how we stay resilient in turbulent times.

Lynnette Khalfani-Cox, bestselling author and financial educator known as The Money Coach:

During the 2007-'09 recession, I remember economists and personal finance folks saying "this time is different." I would actually echo that to people now because this time really is different. The main difference, though, is unstable policy. I know there's a lot going on right now with tariffs, interest rates, inflation, lack of savings, layoffs and more, but I think more than ever, it's a mistake to act rashly just because of the headlines. This observation doesn't mean don't do anything whatsoever. It does mean people should realize that a lot is up in the air and that uncertainty is likely going to dominate the economic environment for quite a long time. So perhaps this time around - with the threat of a recession - maybe it's best to be more conservative in terms of managing your finances in a way that lets you sleep at night, regardless of what goes on in Washington, D.C.

Jason Cochran, award-winning author of "Here Lies America" and many other travel books:

Buy local, not corporate. You'll meet more people where you live, you'll help invest in the future of your own community instead of sending your dollars to distant investors who are behind many of our current problems, and your monthly expenses will probably go down because you'll be more mindful about your purchases. The same goes for travel: Travel domestically, because if you do, it's easier to vacation on a budget, and at the same time, your appreciation for your own country will be enriched.

Josh Smith, senior manager of e-commerce at Newsweek:

During the 2007-'09 recession, my best advice was often about preserving cash flow and minimizing recurring expenses. Even after eliminating unneeded subscriptions, you can lower what you pay for essentials like your cellphone and internet service. A brief, 15-minutes phone call with your provider can often result in a lower bill and a rate lock. I did this to cut my home internet bill in half, and locked in the rate for several years. If this doesn't work, it's time to look for a competing service. It's best to do this soon, before all the prices start to go up again.

Ann Brenoff, AARP contributor and author of "Caregivers Are Mad as Hell!: Rants from the Wife of the Very Sick Man in Room 5029":

I was months away from turning 60 when I was laid off from the Los Angeles Times in the 2007-'09 recession. I was the sole support for my family - two minor children and a sick husband. The first thing I did was ask myself, "What else can I do with my skills?" My other best advice: Stay confident. Don't devalue your worth, and network until you drop. Text everyone who you know who works doing what you want to do or for a company you want to work for. Most of all, do not blame yourself. There are forces of nature that are beyond our control. Believe you will succeed and you will.

Bruce Watson, writer and editor who lives and works in New York City:

I think my biggest piece of advice at the time (and one I was certainly using myself!) was to eat like a peasant. Peasant foods use lower-cost ingredients that are often higher-flavor, and make up for lower-quality cuts of meat by slow-cooking them. Whether you're talking about goulash or Italian white bean soup or marinara sauce or paella, the food of the working class tends to have a lot more character, is healthier and is lower cost.

Julia Scott Cavanaugh, formerly known as Bargain Babe, now running a digital marketing agency:

The best advice I gave out during the 2007-'09 recession is to track your daily spending. We obsess over retirement funds, but we spend the vast majority of our money on daily purchases. If we can understand where to trim on a daily basis, we stand to save a lot.

Tom Barlow, fiction writer and poet:

The real cost of driving can explode a budget; it's not just the cost of gas or electricity, but also maintenance, insurance, financing, taxes and the biggest hidden cost, depreciation. According to AAA, for the average SUV that can add up to 78 cents a mile. Are you really saving money driving across town to Costco when you could walk to the corner Mom and Pop?

Julie Tilsner, semiretired editor and writer:

Last time this happened, I remember reading in real time about Lehman Bros. abruptly going bankrupt, and how that proved to be the first domino to fall. Here are my recommendations from back then for this coming downturn: Cut all extraneous expenses (subscriptions, streaming services, housecleaning, daily Starbucks, etc.). Create weekly meal plans to streamline food purchases and decrease waste. Leftovers become lunches the next day. Pay down debt now while you still have a job. Do not buy more stuff. Make do with what you have or what you can borrow from neighbors. (And get to know them all...mutual aid will help everyone get through this time.) Stop consuming and hunker down.

Aaron Crowe, freelance writer then and now:

The advice that I think had the most impact back in 2008 was to be prepared for a job loss and what to do immediately after one. Too many people I knew who were laid off in 2008 and they didn't apply for unemployment benefits as soon as they could. Some employers told their workers that they couldn't collect these benefits until after their severance package was spent. They're wrong. The sooner you apply, the sooner you'll get the money you're entitled to. Unemployment checks helped me get back on my feet until I could find part-time work as a freelance writer. Once things improved, I started putting money away in an emergency fund to keep my family afloat for up to a year.

Beth Pinsker, now writing the Fix My Portfolio column at MarketWatch:

The thing I tried most to get across with WalletPop is that money is involved in everything we do in life, from the kind of shampoo you use to how you manage your retirement funds, and is at the base of every news story. The best advice that came out of that was that being a good consumer is not all about deprivation and dourness. So if there are hard days ahead, enjoy a cup of stew with ingredients you bought on sale from a local market while you watch a video about how to change the air filter in your used car. And know that we all have more resources for strength and resilience than we realize.

Got a question about investing, how it fits into your overall financial plan and what strategies can help you make the most out of your money? You can write to me at beth.pinsker@marketwatch.com. Please put "Fix My Portfolio" in the subject line.

You can also join the Retirement conversation in our Facebook community: Retire Better with MarketWatch.

By submitting your story to Dow Jones & Co., the publisher of MarketWatch, you understand and agree that we may use your story, or versions of it, in all media and platforms, including via third parties.

More Fix My Portfolio

-- As the S&P 500 crashes, this new retiree says advisers are 'head-in-the-sand clueless'

-- 'Stay the course' sounds like advice for rich investors. What can I do now?

-- As tariffs make car prices soar, dealers are clamoring for my used Honda Fit. Should I sell it?

-Beth Pinsker

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  04-08-25 1803ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Excelerate Energy Announces First Quarter 2025 Earnings Conference Call Date
data.description:
Excelerate Energy Announces First Quarter 2025 Earnings Conference Call Date

Excelerate Energy, Inc. (NYSE: EE) (the “Company” or “Excelerate”) will release its first quarter 2025 results on Wednesday, May 7, 2025, following the close of U.S. financial markets. The earnings release and presentation for the first quarter 2025 results will be available on the investor page of the Company’s website at www.excelerateenergy.com.

On Thursday, May 8, 2025, the Company’s management team will host a conference call for analysts and investors at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time (7:30 a.m. Central Time). The call will also be webcast live at www.excelerateenergy.com. An archived replay of the call and a copy of the presentation will be on the website following the call.

About Excelerate Energy:

Excelerate Energy, Inc. is a U.S.-based LNG company located in The Woodlands, Texas. Excelerate is changing the way the world accesses cleaner forms of energy by providing integrated services along the LNG value chain with an objective of delivering rapid-to-market and reliable LNG solutions to customers. The Company offers a full range of flexible regasification services from FSRUs to infrastructure development to LNG supply. Excelerate has a presence in Abu Dhabi, Antwerp, Boston, Buenos Aires, Chattogram, Dhaka, Doha, Dubai, Hanoi, Helsinki, London, Rio de Janeiro, Singapore, and Washington, DC. For more information, please visit www.excelerateenergy.com.

Investors:

Excelerate Energy

data.title: Textron Aviation Expands Affordable and Convenient Health Care Options for Wichita Employees With Second Onsite Plane Healthy Wellness Center & Pharmacy
data.description:
Textron Aviation Expands Affordable and Convenient Health Care Options for Wichita Employees With Second Onsite Plane Healthy Wellness Center & Pharmacy

Textron Aviation Inc., a Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT) company, today announced the opening of its second innovative Plane Healthy Wellness Center & Pharmacy, located at the company’s east headquarters in Wichita, Kansas. The Wichita-based wellness center provides more than 9,500 employees and their dependents the choice to access affordable, convenient, comprehensive and quality health care services, helping to manage healthcare costs and giving them a resource to improve their overall wellbeing.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250408256145/en/

Textron Aviation expands affordable and convenient health care options for Wichita employees with second onsite Plane Healthy Wellness Center & Pharmacy

The Plane Healthy Wellness Center & Pharmacy is a private clinic focused on the needs of Textron Aviation employees and their dependents, leading to a personalized experience and shorter wait times. The wellness center is owned by Textron Aviation and operated and staffed by Premise Health, a leading provider of employer-direct healthcare services in the nation.

Textron Aviation opened this facility following the success of its first location at its west Wichita campus, which opened in 2022. Last year, the west Wichita wellness center served employees and their families with more than 19,000 visits, filling over 32,000 prescriptions and achieving a world-class Net Promoter Score of 90 — significantly surpassing the healthcare industry average of 58.

“At Textron Aviation, we embody our value of 'Be Human' — putting people at the forefront of everything we do,” said Maggie Topping, senior vice president, Human Resources & Communications, Textron Aviation. “Our employees are our greatest asset, and we are dedicated to providing them with the resources they need to thrive both personally and professionally. By opening our second wellness center, we are making a significant investment in our people, ensuring they have easy access to convenient and affordable care.”

Onsite services include primary care, urgent care, pharmacy with drive-through access, physical therapy, radiology, mental health services, wellness coaching and condition management. For added convenience, virtual appointments are also available during regular business hours at the same cost as in-person visits.

Learn more about Textron Aviation's Total Rewards benefits beginning day one and join the team at txtav.com/careers.

About Textron Aviation
We inspire the journey of flight. For more than 95 years, Textron Aviation has empowered our collective talent across the Beechcraft, Cessna and Hawker brands to design and deliver the best aviation experience for our customers. With a range that includes everything from business jets, turboprops, and high-performance pistons, to special missions, military trainer and defense products, Textron Aviation has the most versatile and comprehensive aviation product portfolio in the world and a workforce that has produced more than half of all general aviation aircraft worldwide. Customers in more than 170 countries rely on our legendary performance, reliability and versatility, along with our trusted global customer service network, for affordable, productive and flexible flight.

For more information, visit www.txtav.com | specialmissions.txtav.com | defense.txtav.com | scorpion.txtav.com.

About Textron
Textron is a multi-industry company that leverages its global network of aircraft, defense, industrial and finance businesses to provide customers with innovative solutions and services. Textron is known around the world for its powerful brands such as Bell, Cessna, Beechcraft, Pipistrel, Jacobsen, Kautex, Lycoming, E-Z-GO, Arctic Cat, and Textron Systems.

For more information, visit: www.textron.com.

Certain statements in this press release are forward-looking statements which may project revenues or describe strategies, goals, outlook or other non-historical matters; these statements speak only as of the date on which they are made, and we undertake no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements. These statements are subject to known and unknown risks, uncertainties, and other factors that may cause our actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements, including, but not limited to, changes

data.title: Cholula® and Taco Cabana Ignite Taste Buds with Bold New Partnership Ahead of Cinco de Mayo
data.description:
Cholula® and Taco Cabana Ignite Taste Buds with Bold New Partnership Ahead of Cinco de Mayo

Spicy Collaboration Brings a Fiery Twist to TC’s Signature Margaritas

Two legendary flavors are joining forces to set taste buds ablaze. Taco Cabana, the beloved destination for bold, Mexican-inspired cuisine, is thrilled to announce an exciting partnership with Cholula®, makers of the #1 Mexican Hot Sauce in the World*. Together, they are launching an exclusive new cocktail that’s guaranteed to spice up happy hour: the Cholula® Rita.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250408658482/en/

Cholula® and Taco Cabana Ignite Taste Buds with Bold New Partnership Ahead of Cinco de Mayo

Fusing the balanced heat of Cholula with the classic zest of a margarita, this innovative cocktail blends TC’s signature frozen margarita, tropical flavors, and Cholula Chili Lime Hot Sauce. The flavors pair beautifully with the 100% blue agave, additive-free Casa Alta Tequila currently available only at Taco Cabana. The result? A perfectly balanced sip that delivers a bold, tangy, and refreshing finish—making it the ultimate pairing for Taco Cabana’s legendary tacos, fajitas, chips & queso and more.

“Taco Cabana has always been about pushing the boundaries of flavor,” said Ulyses Camacho, President & COO of Taco Cabana. “Partnering with Cholula allows us to bring their authentic Mexican flavor to an entirely new experience—one that perfectly complements our delicious food. The generations-old family recipe that continues to feature arbol and piquin peppers adds a new dimension to the classic margarita.”

This collaboration is rolling out at all Texas Taco Cabana locations starting April 30, 2025, just in time to celebrate Cinco de Mayo.

“We know our guests love bold flavors, and this partnership is all about delivering an exciting new way to enjoy their favorite margarita,” said Camacho. “The Cholula® Rita is the perfect fusion of heat and refreshment, and we can’t wait for our customers to experience this one-of-a-kind creation.”

For more information about this sizzling collaboration, follow Cholula and Taco Cabana on social media using #CholulaRita.

*Source: Euromonitor International Limited; based on custom research conducted September 2024 for value sales in 2023 through all retail channels. Mexican hot sauce defined as spicy table sauce/condiment that are manufactured in Mexico.

Cholula® is a registered trademark of Spicy Liquid LLC, used under license.

About Taco Cabana:

Taco Cabana, a subsidiary of YTC Enterprises, LLC, was founded in 1978. The brand specializes in Mexican-inspired food including breakfast, lunch and dinner tacos, dozen taco boxes, enchiladas, fajitas, quesadillas, flautas, burritos, double crunch pizzas, freshly-made flour tortillas and a selection of made-from-scratch salsas and sauces. Restaurants feature open-display cooking, a selection of bottled beer and signature tequila margaritas, patio dining, drive-thru windows, curbside pick-up and delivery. As of April 2, 2025, Taco Cabana operates 141 company-owned restaurants in Texas. For more info, visit tacocabana.com.

Taco Cabana Media Relations:
Kristyna Otto
Powerhouse+Co.
kristyna@powerhouseandco.com
949.261.2216
